seed_expert_discoveries.project: Prompting Technique: Frame Tasks as Questions
seed_expert_discoveries.technical_takeaway: Appending '?' to prompts invites models to push back, rate idea quality, and suggest alternatives instead of blindly executing, improving alignment with intent over 'plan mode'.

global_discoveries.technical_takeaway: Open-source persistent cross-session memory system for 10+ AI coding agents (Claude, Cursor, etc.) using auto-hooks, BM25+vector+graph retrieval, 4-tier consolidation; achieves 95.2% recall, 98% token cost reduction vs LLM summaries, SQLite-based with no external deps.
New repo for Hermes-style local agent instances on Nemotron, focused on persistence, memory-awareness, and extensibility for local-first workflows and multi-agent testing.
Graph-based memory system for autonomous AI agents with bio-inspired sleep-based consolidation, powered by KIP & AndaDB for semantic recall and long-term agent memory.

near_misses.technical_takeaway: Enhanced skills hub search and agent harness integration for comprehensive info and better agent workflows.
near_misses.reason: Relevant to agent tools but incremental update rather than new framework/repo/breakthrough.

near_misses.technical_takeaway: Memory and personalization layers for agents with accuracy/cost benchmarks.
near_misses.reason: Discusses existing projects and benchmarks but no new open-source release or core innovation detailed.

notes: Seed experts had limited high-signal technical posts in the window (mostly non-technical). Global search surfaced several memory-focused agent repos/tools as the strongest matches for practical, actionable AI innovations. No major new orchestration frameworks or efficiency breakthroughs identified.
